body_txt: The opposite of a Genius Ditz , this character proves competent at almost everything... except their actual job. A sort of adult Book Dumb , the main question on everybody's minds, in- or out-of-universe, is "How on earth did this fool get hired, especially given there are other jobs they would be far more competent at!?" (Though sometimes their Blue Blood may give you a reason to suspect nepotism .) Different from Fake Ultimate Hero in that the latter at least puts on a ruse of being competent that could actually fool someone. When this character's actual job seems to be nonexistent, they are one of The Pirates Who Don't Do Anything . Like the Pointy-Haired Boss , may be a result of The Peter Principle , where they have been promoted from a position which they mastered to one which they have not, or The Dilbert Principle , where someone's been Kicked Upstairs to get them away from the workflow. Compare and contrast Genius Ditz , which is a character who is ditzy and unskilled except for being brilliant in their actual job, and Bunny-Ears Lawyer , whose quirks belie their true competence. This can also be combined with High Hopes, Zero Talent when they're working at their dream job. Contrast I'm a Doctor, Not a Placeholder and the Almighty Janitor . Also contrast Crippling Overspecialization , where they only excel at one function and are useless at anything else. Despite the name, this character can be a civilian with no military title. A Modern Major General who actually is a military officer may overlap with General Failure . If you were looking for the song and works which parody it, see Major General Song . See also Armchair Military , which the Trope Namer parodies.
